steps.title: Identify the antecedent and grammatical need
steps.content: The blank in "______ peer autonomous Soviet socialist republics" requires a possessive. The antecedent is "Mari Autonomous Soviet Socialist Republic" (singular).

steps.title: Apply pronoun-antecedent agreement
steps.content:
Identify the grammar issue: possessive pronoun vs. contraction. Rule: a possessive pronoun has no apostrophe; a contraction with an apostrophe means "it is" or "they are," not ownership.
  • Choice A — "they're": contraction of "they are"; not a possessive and is plural. ✗
  • Choice B — "its": singular possessive pronoun; correctly refers to the singular antecedent. ✓
  • Choice C — "their": plural possessive; does not agree with singular antecedent. ✗
  • Choice D — "it's": contraction of "it is"; not a possessive. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
